svg.text: • 268. Missing Number — values 0..n, find the single missing one
• 448. Find All Numbers Disappeared in an Array — values 1..n, return absent
• 287. Find the Duplicate Number — values 1..n, exactly one duplicate
• 442. Find All Duplicates in an Array — every value appears once or twice
• 41. First Missing Positive — hardest; ignore values outside 1..n during placement
• 645. Set Mismatch — find the duplicated and the missing pair simultaneously
Why cyclic sort beats alternatives here
• Hashset: O(n) time, O(n) space — fails the O(1) extra-space constraint
• Sort then scan: O(n log n) time — slower than necessary
• XOR trick: works for single missing, not duplicates or multi-missing
• Negation marking: also O(n)/O(1), but mutates sign — fragile if values can be 0
Cyclic sort: O(n) time, O(1) extra space, generalises to all six problems above.
When values are bounded by 1..n, cyclic sort is the default tool.
